operating_state: Consolidated revenues grew 13% to $80.3 billion driven by UnitedHealthcare serving 1.6 million additional people through Medicare Advantage and Medicaid expansion, and Optum businesses growing 18%. UnitedHealthcare revenues increased 12% with Medicare Advantage membership rising 9% and Medicaid enrollment up 12%, while Optum Health revenues surged 32% from value-based care arrangements and business combinations. Earnings from operations increased 19% to $7.1 billion.
cost_structure: Medical care ratio improved to 81.5% from 82.8% due to COVID-19 effects and business mix changes, while operating cost ratio increased slightly to 14.6% from business mix and investments. Medical costs increased 12% driven by membership growth across all segments. Operating costs rose 13% reflecting business expansion and strategic investments.
material_events: The company issued $6 billion in long-term debt across five tranches with maturities from 2027 to 2062 for general corporate purposes. Board increased quarterly dividend to $6.60 annually from $5.80 per share. The company repurchased 10 million shares for $5 billion during the six-month period. UnitedHealth Group and Change Healthcare waived merger agreement termination rights until December 31, 2022.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating uncertainty in care patterns with overall utilization near normal baselines but varying by care type. Future care patterns may moderately exceed normal baselines as previously deferred care is obtained, with potential temporary acuity increases from missed regular care. Disrupted care patterns affect member health status information collection, impacting revenue in risk adjustment businesses.
forward_outlook: Management anticipates pending business combinations including Change Healthcare and LHC Group requiring approximately $14 billion in capital excluding disposition proceeds. The company maintains authorization to repurchase up to 35 million additional shares and expects continued growth across both platforms. Strategic focus remains on expanding value-based care arrangements and technology-enabled services.
uncertainty_profile: Ultimate COVID-19 impact remains uncertain depending on pandemic duration, variant severity, vaccination effectiveness and economic conditions. Pending regulatory approvals for major acquisitions create execution risk. The company faces ongoing DOJ legal action relating to risk adjustment submission matters and potential changes in Medicare programs and risk adjustment audits.
